The paper was published on March 31, 2017, in the Journal of Interdisciplinary Science Topics (Vol. 6). The research investigates the feasibility of the character Hiccup, from the How to Train Your Dragon franchise, being able to supply enough fish to meet the dietary needs of the dragon Toothless during a twelve-day timeframe when Toothless was unable to hunt for himself. 

The study reveals that in the twelve days that Toothless was trapped, he would have required 13 Icelandic cod and 56 Atlantic salmon each day. This would total 716 kg of fish, resulting in Hiccup needing to carry 8,592 kg of fish over the entire period, which is deemed unrealistic given his physical limitations as portrayed in the films. 

The study details assumptions made regarding the circumstances depicted in the films and employs calculations with regard to fish sizes and weights based on the narrative context. In conclusion, it posits that Hiccup could not feasibly sustain Toothless’ diet under the stated conditions.
